title: ulmus × hollandica var. superba
intro: Ulmus × hollandica var. superba, also known as the Dutch elm tree, is a cultivar of the elm tree, specifically bred for its striking, vase-shaped habit and vibrant yellow fall color. Traditionally, the bark and leaves of Ulmus × hollandica var. superba have been used in herbal medicine, particularly for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of Ulmus × hollandica var. superba can be decocted in water or tea to utilize its medicinal properties, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its effectiveness.
